1 HB2720
Legislative Appropriations ($)
MAKING APPROPRIATIONS TO PROVIDE FOR THE EXPENSES OF THE LEGISLATURE, THE AUDITOR, THE LEGISLATIVE REFERENCE BUREAU, THE OMBUDSMAN, AND THE ETHICS COMMISSION.
Appropriates funds to provide for the expenses of the Legislature, Office of the Auditor, Legislative Reference Bureau, Office of the Ombudsman, and State Ethics Commission.		 (S) 2/23/2016- Act 001, 02/23/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1101). SOUKI, FUKUMOTO CHANG, SAIKI WAM
2 SB805
Nursing; Practical Nurse; Registered Nurse
RELATING TO HEALTH.
Removes the sunset provision from Act 255, Session Laws of Hawaii 2012, relating to the definitions of "the practice of nursing as a licensed practical nurse" and "the practice of nursing as a registered nurse".		 (S) 3/28/2016- Act 002, 03/24/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1102). GREEN HLT, CPC
3 SB2825 SD1
Pacific International Space Center for Exploration Systems; Board of Directors Membership
RELATING TO THE PACIFIC INTERNATIONAL SPACE CENTER FOR EXPLORATION SYSTEMS.
Eliminates the ex officio National Aeronautics and Space Administration representative from the Pacific International Space Center for Exploration Systems Board. (SD1)		 (H) 4/4/2016- Act 003, on 03/31/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1103). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) EDB  HB2296
4 SB2310
Domestic Abuse; Mutual Protective Orders
RELATING TO DOMESTIC ABUSE PROTECTIVE ORDERS.
Prohibits the court from granting mutual protective orders unless separate petitions are filed.		 (H) 4/13/2016- Act 004, on 04/11/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1104). L. THIELEN, BAKER, ESPERO, GREEN, KIDANI, SHIMABUKURO, TOKUDA, Inouye, Kim HUS, JUD  HB1905
5 SB2856
Office of Consumer Protection; Service of Process; Subpoena; Investigators
RELATING TO CONSUMER PROTECTION.
Clarifies scope of Office of Consumer Protection investigator powers.		 (H) 4/13/2016- Act 005, on 04/11/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1105). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) JUD  HB2327
6 SB2851 SD1
Property Insurance; Hawaii Property Insurance Association; Lava Zone; State of Emergency; Moratorium; Determination of Availability of Insurance
RELATING TO INSURANCE.
Requires the Insurance Commissioner to determine whether residential property insurance is unavailable due to a moratorium on insurance policies in a lava zone during a state of emergency due to lava flow. (SD1)		 (H) 4/13/2016- Act 006, on 04/11/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1106). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC  HB2322
7 HB2329
Cause of Action; Limitation of Action; Consumer Protection
RELATING TO CONSUMER PROTECTION.
Clarifies that the statute of limitations governing it does not apply to the State and its agencies with respect to chapter 480, Hawaii Revised Statutes. Repeals subsection (b) of section 48024, Hawaii Revised Statutes. Technical amendments.		 (H) 4/13/2016- Act 007, on 04/11/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1107). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) CPH, JDL  SB2858
8 HB2331 HD1
Board of Nursing; Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology; Board Member Terms
RELATING TO BOARD MEMBER TERMS FOR THE BOARD OF NURSING AND THE BOARD OF SPEECH PATHOLOGY AND AUDIOLOGY.
Amends the length of terms from three years to four years of the members of the Board of Nursing and the Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology. (HB2331 HD1)		 (H) 4/20/2016- Act 008, on 04/19/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1108). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) CPH  SB2860
9 HB2639
Wireless Telecommunications; Domestic Abuse
RELATING TO WIRELESS TELECOMMUNICATIONS SERVICE.
Requires a wireless telecommunications service provider, upon request by a domestic abuse victim, to: (1) transfer billing authority and all rights to a wireless telephone number to the victim; or (2) remove or release the victim from a shared wireless plan and assign the victim a substitute telephone number without charge, penalty, or fee. Provides that there is no cause of action against a wireless telecommunications service provider, its officers, employees, or agents for transferring billing authority and all rights to a wireless telephone number to a domestic abuse victim.		 (S) 4/22/2016- Act 009, 04/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1109). MORIKAWA, BELATTI, EVANS, HAR, ICHIYAMA, LUKE, SAN BUENAVENTURA, DeCoite, Kobayashi, Lowen CPH, JDL
10 HB2494
Blood Glucose Monitoring; Students; Diabetes
RELATING TO BLOOD GLUCOSE MONITORING.
Authorizes DOE employees to volunteer to administer or assist a student with blood glucose monitoring. 		(S) 4/22/2016- Act 010, 04/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1110). TAKUMI EDU, CPH  SB2975
11 SB2541
Information Technology; Chief Information Officer; Authorization to Use or Transfer Funds; State Budget
RELATING TO INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY.
Authorizes the State CIO to use or transfer funds, appropriated in the 2015 State budget Act, to executive departments and agencies for projects that have been approved by the governor.		 (S) 4/22/2016- Act 011, 04/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1111). TOKUDA, KIDANI, Dela Cruz EDB, FIN
12 HB2422 HD1
Liquor License; Liquor Commission
RELATING TO LIQUOR.
Amends liquor license application and operating procedures to allow publicly-traded companies, or entities ultimately solely owned by a publicly-traded company, to provide information for only those officers designated as primary decisionmakers regarding the purchase and sale of liquor and clarifies that the requirement of prior approval from a county liquor commission regarding a change in ownership of at least twenty-five percent of a corporation's outstanding capital stock applies only to voting stock. Allows the applicant for a liquor license or renewal, or both the transferor and transferee in the case of a liquor license transfer, to present a tax clearance certificate from the Department of Taxation and the Internal Revenue Service evidencing that the applicant, transferor, and transferee do not owe the State or federal government any delinquent taxes, penalties, or interest. (HB2422 HD1)		 (S) 4/22/2016- Act 012, 04/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1112). TSUJI, MIZUNO, Evans, Nakashima PSM, CPH
13 SB2775
State Innovation Waiver, Affordable Care Act
RELATING TO THE AFFORDABLE CARE ACT SECTION 1332 STATE INNOVATION WAIVER.
Authorizes the State to submit and implement a waiver from certain provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act of 2010. 		(H) 4/26/2016- Act 013, on 04/25/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1113). BAKER (Introduced by request of another party) HLT/LAB, FIN  HB2478
14 SB2341 SD1
Respiratory Therapists; Regulation
RELATING TO RESPIRATORY THERAPISTS.
Makes permanent the regulation of respiratory therapists under chapter 466D, Hawaii Revised Statutes. (SD1)		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 014, on 04/25/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1114). BAKER, ENGLISH, KEITH-AGARAN, KIDANI, Espero, Gabbard, Ihara, Nishihara, Ruderman HLT, CPC  HB1857
15 SB2934 SD2
Driver License Renewal; REAL ID Act
RELATING TO DRIVER LICENSING.
Ensures driver license procedures are compliant with the REAL ID Act. (SD2)		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 015, on 04/25/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1115). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) TRN, FIN  HB2405
16 SB2312 SD1 HD1
Sexual Conduct; Promotion of Child Abuse; Obscenity
RELATING TO THE PENAL CODE.
Expands the definition of the term "sexual conduct" as that term is used in the offenses of promoting child abuse to include specific types of conduct. Amends the definition of "sexual conduct" as that term is used in the offenses of promoting child abuse and obscenity to repeal references to homosexuality and lesbianism. (SB2312 HD1)		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 016, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1116). L. THIELEN, BAKER, ESPERO, GREEN, INOUYE, SHIMABUKURO, TOKUDA, Kidani, Kim, Riviere JUD  HB1908
17 SB2246
Driver's Licensing; Penalties; Refusal to Submit to Testing
RELATING TO TESTING FOR INTOXICANTS.
Repeals section 291E-68, Hawaii Revised Statutes, regarding refusing to submit to a breath, blood, or urine test for those arrested for the use of intoxicants while operating a vehicle. Makes conforming amendments to section 291E-15, Hawaii Revised Statutes.		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 017, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1117). KEITH-AGARAN, Baker, Dela Cruz, Galuteria, Nishihara, Shimabukuro TRN, JUD  HB1831
18 SB2862
Nursing; Nursys; Licensure; Identifying Information
RELATING TO NURSING.
Allows the Board of Nursing to more fully participate in Nursys, a national database for nurse licensees by sharing license and disciplinary action information.		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 018, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1118). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC, JUD  HB2333
19 SB2813 SD1
Guardianship; Gun Control
RELATING TO GUARDIANSHIP.
Requires the courts to provide information relating to adult guardianships to the Hawaii criminal justice data center to disclose to the Federal Bureau of Investigation National Instant Criminal Background Check System database for gun control purposes. Requires the Hawaii criminal justice data center to maintain orders of appointment or information from orders of appointment for use in firearms permitting and registration. Takes effect January 1, 2017. (SD1)		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 019, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1119). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) JUD  HB2284
20 SB2885 SD1
Birth Defects Program; Use of Data
RELATING TO BIRTH DEFECTS.
Authorizes the use of birth defects data for education of health care providers to reduce morbidity or mortality and to increase physicians' knowledge of resources available for families of persons with birth defects. (SD1)		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 020, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1120). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) HLT  HB2356
21 SB2874
Certified Nurse Aides
RELATING TO CERTIFIED NURSE AIDES.
Repeals section 346-47 and amends sections 457A-1.5, 457A-2, and 457A-3, Hawaii Revised Statutes, to delete references to the Department of Human Services as licensing or certifying health care settings; and deletes references to the Department of Human Services investigating and disciplining certified nurse aides employed in health care settings.		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 021, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1121). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) HUS/HLT, JUD  HB2345
22 SB2873
Orders For Immediate Protection
RELATING TO ORDERS FOR IMMEDIATE PROTECTION.
Amends section 346-231, Hawaii Revised Statutes, to address inconsistencies in statutory requirements regarding orders for immediate protection; and allows the Department of Human Services discretion when to seek an order for immediate protection of vulnerable adults.		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 022, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1122). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) HUS, JUD  HB2344
23 SB2511
Broadband; Broadband Assistance Advisory Council
RELATING TO TELECOMMUNICATIONS.
Includes the Director of Business, Economic Development, and Tourism as a permanent member on the Broadband Assistance Advisory Council. Ensures a member from each county is represented through appointment on the council. Authorizes the chair of the council to designate representatives of other interested public and private sector organizations to serve as members of the council or the work groups of the council on an ad hoc basis as needed.		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 023, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1123). WAKAI, CHUN OAKLAND, INOUYE, KIDANI, Dela Cruz, English, Gabbard, Galuteria, Green, Harimoto, Keith-Agaran, Kim, Nishihara, Riviere, Slom, L. Thielen EDB, CPC  HB2549
24 SB2256 SD1
Driver's License; Korean Conflict Veterans
RELATING TO MOTOR VEHICLE DRIVER LICENSING.
Authorizes issuance of driver's licenses with the notation of "veteran" for persons who are veterans of the Korean conflict and persons who served in the armed forces of the Republic of Korea, who fought under the command of the United Nations led by the United States, during the Korean conflict and are currently United States citizens. (SD1)		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 024, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1124). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) VMI, FIN  HB2196
25 SB305 SD1 HD1
Hawaii Health Systems Corporation; Electronic Smoking Devices; Tobacco Products; Prohibition
RELATING TO USE OF TOBACCO PRODUCTS AND E-CIGARETTES ON HAWAII HEALTH SYSTEMS CORPORATION PREMISES.
Prohibits the use of tobacco products and electronic smoking devices on the premises of Hawaii Health Systems Corporation facilities. (SB305 HD1)		 (H) 4/26/2016- Act 025, on 04/26/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1125). GREEN, BAKER, CHUN OAKLAND, HARIMOTO, RUDERMAN, WAKAI, Espero, Gabbard, Ihara, Keith-Agaran, Riviere, Shimabukuro CPH
26 HB939
Amendments to Birth Certificates, Establishment of Parenthood
RELATING TO AMENDING IDENTITY OF REGISTRANT'S PARENT ON A BIRTH CERTIFICATE.
Clarifies that amendments to birth records that change parenthood shall not be conducted through the Uniform Information Practices Act procedures to correct personal records, but must be pursuant to a court order of appropriate jurisdiction or other legal establishment of parenthood.		 (S) 4/28/2016- Act 026, 04/27/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1126). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) CPH, JDL  SB1108
27 HB2416
Renewable Energy Facility Siting Process; Repeal
RELATING TO RENEWABLE ENERGY.
Repeals chapter 201N, HRS, relating to the REFSP. Deposits proceeds in the renewable energy facility siting special fund into the general fund.		 (S) 4/28/2016- Act 027, 04/27/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1127). JORDAN, YAMASHITA, CULLEN, LUKE, NISHIMOTO, DeCoite TRE, WAM
28 SB2107
Honolulu Prosecuting Attorney Package; Pen Register; Definition
RELATING TO PEN REGISTERS.
Amends the definition of "pen register" to include cell phones and other communications devices among those that the numbers transmitted to and from may be recorded.		 (S) 4/28/2016- Act 028, 04/28/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1128). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC, JUD  HB1663
29 SB2333 SD1
Marriage and Family Therapists; Licensed Marriage and Family Therapists
RELATED TO LICENSED MARRIAGE AND FAMILY THERAPISTS.
Permits marriage and family therapists licensed pursuant to chapter 451J, Hawaii Revised Statutes, to use the title "licensed marriage and family therapist". Makes conforming amendments. (SD1)		 (S) 4/28/2016- Act 029, 04/28/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1129). BAKER, ENGLISH, KEITH-AGARAN, KIDANI, RUDERMAN, Espero, Gabbard, Ihara, Nishihara, Taniguchi CPC, JUD  HB1629
30 SB2838 SD1
Hawaii Employer-Union Health Benefits Trust Fund
RELATING TO INVESTMENTS OF THE HAWAII EMPLOYER-UNION HEALTH BENEFITS TRUST FUND.
Repeals certain restrictions on investments that can be made by the Hawaii Employer-Union Health Benefits Trust Fund. (SD1)		 (S) 4/28/2016- Act 030, 04/28/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1130). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) LAB, FIN  HB2309
31 SB2841
Employees' Retirement System; Employer Retirement Contributions
RELATING TO PAYMENTS BY EMPLOYERS TO THE EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM.
Confirms that employer contributions to the Employees' Retirement System may be made in advance or on a monthly basis.		 (S) 4/28/2016- Act 031, 04/28/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1131). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) LAB, FIN  HB2312
32 SB2887 SD1
Developmental Disabilities
RELATING TO DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES.
Amends the definition of "developmental disabilities" in section 333F-1, Hawaii Revised Statutes, to include children from birth to age 9 who have a substantial developmental delay or specific congenital or acquired condition and without services and supports, have a high probability of meeting criteria for developmental disabilities later in life. (SD1)		 (S) 4/28/2016- Act 032, 04/28/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1132). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) HUS/HLT, FIN  HB2358
33 SB2921 HD1
Conformity to the Internal Revenue Code for 2015; Income Tax; Estate and Generation-skipping Transfer Tax
RELATING TO CONFORMITY TO THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE.
Conforms Hawaii income and estate and generation-skipping transfer tax laws to the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended as of December 31, 2015, and makes technical, nonsubstantive amendments. (SB2921 HD1)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 033, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1133). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) FIN  HB2392
34 SB2916 SD1 HD1
Public Safety; Firearms; Parole; Reincarceration
RELATING TO PUBLIC SAFETY.
Includes misdemeanor offenses under chapter 134, Hawaii Revised Statutes, relating to firearms, ammunition, and dangerous weapons, to the offenses that disqualify retaken and reimprisoned parolees from provisions regarding reincarceration and credit for time served. (SB2916 HD1)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 034, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1134). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) PBS, JUD  HB2387
35 SB2914 SD1 HD1
Correctional Facilities; Court Orders to Provide Medical Treatment
RELATING TO COURT ORDERS TO PROVIDE MEDICAL TREATMENT IN CORRECTIONAL FACILITIES.
Amends procedures and criteria for involuntary medical treatment of inmates and detainees in correctional facilities by court order. (SB2914 HD1)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 035, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1135). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) PBS/JUD, FIN  HB2385
36 SB2912 SD2 HD1
Statewide Integrated Sex Offender Treatment Program
RELATING TO THE STATEWIDE INTEGRATED SEX OFFENDER TREATMENT PROGRAM.
Aligns state programs with nationally recognized best practices in the assessment, evaluation, treatment, and supervision of adult and certain juvenile sex offenders. Identifies the coordinating body for the sex offender treatment program as the "Sex Offender Management Team" that may consider individual patient files in meetings that are closed to the public. (SB2912 HD1)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 036, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1136). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) JUD  HB2383
37 SB2906 HD2
Threatened and Endangered Species
RELATING TO SECTION 13 OF ACT 380, SESSION LAWS OF HAWAII 1997.
Removes the sunset date on the use of new safe harbor agreements, habitat conservation plans, and incidental take licenses as recovery options for conserving and protecting the State's threatened and endangered species. Requires a biennial report. (SB2906 HD2)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 037, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1137). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) EEP, JUD, FIN  HB2377
38 SB2675 SD1 HD2
Licensing; Reciprocal Discipline; Board of Dental Examiners; Hawaii Medical Board; Board of Nursing; Board of Pharmacy
RELATING TO LICENSING.
Authorizes the Board of Dental Examiners, Hawaii Medical Board, Board of Nursing, and Board of Pharmacy to deny a license to an applicant or impose disciplinary action against a licensee who has been disciplined by another state or a federal agency. Prohibits a licensee from practicing until a final order of discipline is issued if the licensee has been prohibited from practicing in another state. (SB2675 HD2)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 038, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1138). BAKER, ESPERO, INOUYE, RUDERMAN, SHIMABUKURO, TOKUDA, Kidani, Riviere HLT, CPC, JUD  HB2430
39 SB2660 HD2
Deregistration; Bureau of Conveyances; Fee Non-time Share Interests
RELATING TO BUREAU OF CONVEYANCES.
Removes the sunset date of Act 119, Session Laws of Hawaii 2013, as amended, thereby making permanent the voluntary deregistration process for fee non-time share interests. (HD2)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 039, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1139). BAKER, ENGLISH, ESPERO, KEITH-AGARAN, KIDANI, TOKUDA, Ihara, Shimabukuro WAL, CPC, FIN  HB1628
40 SB2607 SD2 HD2
Student Data; Computer Services; Information Protection
RELATING TO STUDENT DATA MANAGEMENT.
Limits the ways in which the operator of a website, online service, online application, or mobile application working with the Department of Education can use student data. (SB2607 HD2)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 040, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1140). KIDANI, DELA CRUZ, HARIMOTO, NISHIHARA, WAKAI, Shimabukuro EDN, CPC, JUD  HB1712
41 SB2565
State Housing Sales Program; Repeal
RELATING TO THE HAWAII PUBLIC HOUSING AUTHORITY.
Repeals the state housing sales program of the Hawaii public housing authority.		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 041, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1141). TOKUDA, BAKER, DELA CRUZ, ENGLISH, GALUTERIA, INOUYE, KIDANI, KOUCHI, NISHIHARA HSG, FIN
42 SB2553 HD1
Non-General Funds; Repeal; Transfer of Balances
RELATING TO NON-GENERAL FUNDS.
Repeals and transfers the unencumbered balances of various non-general funds and accounts recommended by the Auditor in Auditor's Report No. 15-19. (SB2553 HD1)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 042, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1142). TOKUDA, BAKER, DELA CRUZ, ENGLISH, GALUTERIA, HARIMOTO, INOUYE, KEITH-AGARAN, KIDANI, NISHIHARA, SHIMABUKURO FIN
43 SB2552 SD1 HD1
Non-General Funds; Repeal; Transfer of Balances
RELATING TO NON-GENERAL FUNDS.
Repeals and abolishes various non-general funds and accounts, as recommended by the Auditor in Auditor's Report No. 15-17, and transfers unencumbered balances. Requires the Department of Health to conduct a study on the distribution of revenues derived from fees for birth, marriage, divorce, or death certificates. (SB2552 HD1)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 043, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1143). TOKUDA, BAKER, DELA CRUZ, ENGLISH, GALUTERIA, HARIMOTO, INOUYE, KEITH-AGARAN, KIDANI, NISHIHARA, SHIMABUKURO FIN
44 SB2383 SD2 HD2
Hawaii Health Connector; Repeal; Transition
RELATING TO THE HAWAII HEALTH CONNECTOR.
Repeals the Hawaii Health Connector (Connector) as part of the transition to a federally supported, state-based exchange. Specifies that any debts and liabilities of the Connector remain that of the Connector and do not constitute the debts and liabilities of the State. Specifies that the provisions in the measure shall not diminish or limit the consumer protections contained in or alter the provisions of the Hawaii Prepaid Health Care Act. (SB2383 HD2)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 044, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1144). BAKER, INOUYE, KEITH-AGARAN, KIDANI, RIVIERE, TOKUDA, Ruderman HLT, CPC, FIN  HB2479
45 SB2163 SD2 HD2
Service of Process; Water Pollution; Solid Waste Pollution; Notice
RELATING TO SERVICE OF PROCESS.
Allows notice of a water pollution and solid waste pollution violation to be given via a posting on a searchable government website and a sign conspicuously posted on the property, if appropriate, when attempts to serve the notice in person and by mail have been unsuccessful. (SB2163 HD2)		 (S) 5/2/2016- Act 045, 04/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1145). SHIMABUKURO, GREEN, INOUYE, RUDERMAN, Espero, Gabbard, Wakai WAL, JUD  HB1933
46 SB2315 SD2 HD2
Breastfeeding; Jury Duty; Exemption
RELATING TO JURY DUTY.
Exempts from jury duty a woman who is breastfeeding or expressing breast milk for a period of two years from the birth of the child. (SB2315 HD2)		 (S) 5/4/2016- Act 046, 05/03/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1147). SHIMABUKURO, GREEN, Baker, Espero, Inouye, Kidani, Kim, Tokuda HUS, JUD  HB2585
47 SB3126 SD2 HD2 CD1
Public Schools; Emergency Appropriation; Air Conditioning; General Funds ($)
RELATING TO PUBLIC SCHOOLS.
Provides for air conditioning, heat abatement, and related energy efficiency measures at public schools by appropriating general funds to the DOE for these projects. (CD1)		 (S) 5/11/2016- Act 047, 05/05/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1148). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) EEP/EDN, FIN  HB2726
48 HB1581 HD2 SD2 CD1
Judicial Proceedings; Contested Case Hearings; Supreme Court; Expedited Review
RELATING TO JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS.
Requires decisions in contested case hearings of the commission on water resource management, land use commission, public utilities commission, Hawaii community development authority, and those involving conservation districts to be appealed directly to the supreme court, with certain exceptions. Requires the judiciary to submit a report to the legislature. Effective 8/1/2016. Repeals on 7/1/2019. (CD1)		 (S) 5/11/2016- Act 048, 05/10/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1149). RHOADS JUD, WAL
49 HB2308 HD1 SD1
Appropriations for Collective Bargaining Cost Items ($)
MAKING APPROPRIATIONS FOR COLLECTIVE BARGAINING COST ITEMS.
Provides fund authorizations and appropriations for collective bargaining cost items for Unit (11) and their excluded counterparts, including the cost of salary adjustments negotiated between the State and the bargaining unit representative for fiscal biennium 20152017. (SD1)		 (S) 5/11/2016- Act 049, 05/10/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1150). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) JDL, WAM  SB2837

(SB2836 HD2)		 (S) 5/11/2016- Act 050, 05/10/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1151). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) LAB, FIN  HB2307

Real Property Tax
RELATING TO AMENDING OR REPEALING HAWAII REAL PROPERTY TAX LAWS FOR THE PURPOSE OF DELETING OBSOLETE OR UNNECESSARY PROVISIONS.
Repeals various sections of the real property tax laws for the purpose of deleting obsolete and unnecessary provisions. (SD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 052, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1153). LUKE WAM
53 HB2218 HD1 SD1

RELATING TO AMENDING OR REPEALING HAWAII INCOME TAX LAWS FOR THE PURPOSE OF DELETING OBSOLETE OR UNNECESSARY PROVISIONS.

54 HB2033 HD1 SD1
Commission on Water Resource Management; Director of Health Designee
RELATING TO THE COMMISSION ON WATER RESOURCE MANAGEMENT.
Allows the Director of Health to appoint a designee to serve as an ex officio voting member on the Commission on Water Resource Management. (SD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 054, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1155). YAMANE WLA
55 HB2448 HD1 SD1
Revision Bill
RELATING TO STATUTORY REVISION: AMENDING VARIOUS PROVISIONS OF THE HAWAII REVISED STATUTES AND THE SESSION LAWS OF HAWAII FOR THE PURPOSES OF CORRECTING ERRORS AND REFERENCES, CLARIFYING LANGUAGE, AND DELETING OBSOLETE OR UNNECESSARY PROVISIONS.
Amends various provisions of the Hawaii Revised Statutes and the Session Laws of Hawaii for the purposes of correcting errors and references, clarifying language, and deleting obsolete or unnecessary provisions. (SD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 055, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1156). SOUKI JDL
56 SB2121 SD1 HD1 CD1
Public Meetings; Sunshine Law; County Council Members
RELATING TO PUBLIC AGENCY MEETINGS AND RECORDS.
Repeals the June 30, 2016, sunset date of limited statutory exemptions from open meeting requirements which permit county council members to attend public meetings of other boards or community groups. Requires the county councils to submit annual reports to the Legislature on the effectiveness of procedural requirements to effectuate the exemption. (CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 056, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1157). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) JUD  HB1644
57 HB2362 HD2 SD1 CD1
Workforce Development Council; Composition; Duties
RELATING TO THE HAWAII WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT COUNCIL.
Conforms the State Workforce Development Council Law to new federal requirements in the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act of 2014, which supersedes the Workforce Investment Act of 1998. (HB2362 CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 057, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1158). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) JDL  SB2891
58 SB2807 SD2
Consolidation of the Office of Information Management and Technology and the Information and Communication Services Division
RELATING TO ENTERPRISE TECHNOLOGY SERVICES.
Consolidates the Office of Information Management and Technology and the Information and Communication Services Division of the Department of Accounting and General Services, including all related information technology functions, in the Office of Enterprise Technology Services under the direction of the Chief Information Officer. Takes effect on 7/2/2016. (SD2)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 058, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1159). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC, FIN  HB2278
59 SB2213 SD2 HD1 CD1
Nursing Facility Sustainability Program; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO THE NURSING FACILITY SUSTAINABILITY PROGRAM.
Continues the nursing facility sustainability program for one additional year. Appropriates funds out of the nursing facility sustainability program special fund for fiscal year 2016-2017. (CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 059, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1160). CHUN OAKLAND, BAKER, GREEN, NISHIHARA HLT/HUS, FIN  HB1942
60 SB2330 SD2 HD2 CD1
Hospital Sustainability Program; Extension; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO THE HOSPITAL SUSTAINABILITY PROGRAM.
Extends the hospital sustainability program for an additional year. Amends the purposes for which moneys in the hospital sustainability program special fund, may be used. Amends the assessment schedule, scope of application, and rates of the hospital sustainability fee. Appropriates funds out of the hospital sustainability program special fund for fiscal year 2016-2017. (CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 060, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1161). BAKER, ENGLISH, GREEN, KEITH-AGARAN, KIDANI, TOKUDA, Espero, Gabbard, Ihara HLT/HUS, FIN  HB1941
61 HB2415 SD1
Non-general Funds; Repeal; Reclassify; Transfer of Balances
RELATING TO NON-GENERAL FUNDS.
Repeals the Kapolei recreational sports complex special fund, reclassifies the prepaid airport use charge fund to a trust fund, and abolishes the photo enforcement revolving fund. Transfers the remaining unencumbered balances to the general fund. (SD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 061, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1162). JORDAN, LUKE, NISHIMOTO, YAMASHITA, DeCoite, Keohokalole WAM
62 HB2037 HD1 SD2 CD1
Appropriation; International Union for Conservation of Nature World Conservation Congress ($)
RELATING TO THE WORLD CONSERVATION CONGRESS.
Appropriates moneys to the DLNR to host the International Union for Conservation of Nature World Conservation Congress meeting at the Hawaii Convention Center on September 1-10, 2016. (HB2037 CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 062, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1163). YAMANE, CREAGAN, CULLEN, KEOHOKALOLE, LOPRESTI, TSUJI, Evans, Morikawa WLA/TSI, WAM
63 HB2279 HD2 SD2 CD1
Appropriations; Claims Against the State ($)
MAKING APPROPRIATIONS FOR CLAIMS AGAINST THE STATE, ITS OFFICERS, OR ITS EMPLOYEES.
Makes appropriations for claims against the State, its officers, and its employees. (HB2279 CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 063, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1164). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) JDL, WAM  SB2808
64 SB2555 HD1 CD1
Emergency and Budget Reserve Fund; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO STATE FUNDS.
Appropriates funds into the Emergency and Budget Reserve Fund for fiscal year 2015-2016 to comply with Article VII, section 6, of the Hawaii State Constitution, which requires, under certain economic conditions, that the legislature provide a tax credit to state taxpayers or make a deposit into one or more funds. (CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 064, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1165). TOKUDA, BAKER, DELA CRUZ, INOUYE, SHIMABUKURO, Gabbard FIN
65 SB3109 SD1 HD1 CD1
High Technology Development Corporation; Manufacturing Development Program; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO MANUFACTURING.
Requires that the fiscal year 2015-2016 appropriation to HTDC for the manufacturing development program shall lapse by 6/30/17 and not at the end of fiscal year 2015-2016. Appropriates funds for the manufacturing development program for the 2016-2017 fiscal year. (CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 065, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1166). WAKAI, CHUN OAKLAND, ESPERO, GALUTERIA, HARIMOTO, INOUYE, NISHIHARA, SHIMABUKURO, Baker, Keith-Agaran, Kidani, Riviere, Slom, Taniguchi EDB, FIN
66 SB3110 SD1 HD1 CD1
Small Business Innovation Research Awards; High Technology Development Corporation; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO TECHNOLOGY.
Requires that the fiscal year 2015-2016 appropriation to DBEDT for HTDC to provide grants to businesses with a federal small business innovation research phase II or III award shall lapse by June 30, 2017, and not at the end of fiscal year 2015-2016. Appropriates funds to DBEDT for HTDC to provide grants to businesses with a federal small business innovation research phase II or III award for fiscal year 2016-2017. (CD1)		 (H) 6/8/2016- Act 066, on 06/06/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1167). WAKAI, BAKER, CHUN OAKLAND, ENGLISH, ESPERO, GALUTERIA, HARIMOTO, INOUYE, KEITH-AGARAN, NISHIHARA, SHIMABUKURO, Kidani, Kim, Riviere, Slom EDB, FIN
67 SB2453 HD1 CD1
Aquatic Resources; Penalties; Educational Class; Community Service
RELATING TO AQUATIC RESOURCES PENALTIES.
Authorizes the court to require a person who violates certain laws pertaining to aquatic resources to: (1) complete an aquatic resources educational class administered by the Department of Land and Natural Resources (DLNR) in addition to or in lieu of paying a monetary fine; and (2) perform community service administered by DLNR in lieu of paying a monetary fine. (CD1)		 (H) 6/17/2016- Act 067, on 06/16/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1168). KEITH-AGARAN, DELA CRUZ, GABBARD, KIDANI, Shimabukuro, Wakai OMH, JUD, FIN  HB2078
68 SB2392 SD2 HD3 CD1
Opioid Antagonist; Naloxone Hydrochloride; Drug Overdose Prevention; Emergency Response; Medical Immunity
RELATING TO OPIOID ANTAGONISTS.
Takes steps to reduce opioid-related drug related overdoses in the State by encouraging the use of opioid antagonists to assist individuals experiencing or at risk of experiencing an opioid-related drug overdose. (CD1)		 (H) 6/17/2016- Act 068, on 06/16/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1169). BAKER, CHUN OAKLAND, GALUTERIA, GREEN, INOUYE, KEITH-AGARAN, KIDANI, RUDERMAN, SHIMABUKURO, English, Gabbard HLT, CPC/JUD, FIN  HB1671
69 HB2252 HD1 SD2 CD1
Hospital Discharge Planning; Caregiver Designation; Health Care
RELATING TO DISCHARGE PLANNING.
Requires hospitals to adopt and maintain written discharge policies consistent with recent updates to federal regulations to support families by enabling patients in inpatient hospitals to designate a caregiver prior to discharge or transfer from the facility. (HB2252 CD1)		 (H) 6/17/2016- Act 069, on 06/16/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1170). BELATTI, CACHOLA, CREAGAN, EVANS, ING, ITO, KOBAYASHI, MIZUNO, MORIKAWA, NAKASHIMA, SAIKI, SAY, SOUKI, TAKAYAMA, TSUJI, Johanson, Luke, McKelvey, Nishimoto, Rhoads CPH/HMS, JDL  SB2397
70 HB2722 HD1 SD1 CD1
Unemployment Insurance; State Additional Benefits; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO UNEMPLOYMENT.
Creates a temporary program limited to Maui County to provide additional benefits to unemployed workers by extending their unemployment insurance benefits. Appropriates funds. (HB2722 CD1)		 (S) 6/17/2016- Act 070, 06/17/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1171). SOUKI, NAKASHIMA JDL, WAM  SB3038
71 HB2605 HD1 SD2 CD1
Hawaiian Commercial & Sugar Company; Makena Beach and Golf Resort; Dislocated Workers; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO LABOR.
Appropriates funds to establish, administer, and support job training and supportive services for individuals who are unemployed and dislocated due to the closure of Hawaiian Commercial & Sugar Company and the Makena Beach and Golf Resort on Maui. (HB2605 CD1)		 (S) 6/17/2016- Act 071, 06/17/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1172). MCKELVEY JDL, WAM  SB3081
72 HB2034 HD1 SD2 CD1
Kaho‘olawe Island Reserve Commission; Self-sufficiency Plan; Appropriation ($)
MAKING AN APPROPRIATION TO THE KAHO‘OLAWE ISLAND RESERVE COMMISSION.
Appropriates funds to the Kaho‘olawe Island Reserve Commission for restoration and preservation projects. Requires submission of a financial self-sufficiency and sustainability plan to the Legislature no later than twenty days prior to the 2017 Regular Session. (HB2034 CD1)		 (S) 6/17/2016- Act 072, 06/17/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1173). YAMANE, CULLEN, DECOITE, ING, MCKELVEY, WOODSON, Souki, Yamashita HWN/WLA, WAM  SB2582
73 HB1111 HD2 SD1 CD1
Hawaii Health Systems Corporation; Copies of Records; Fees
RELATING TO RECORDS OF THE HAWAII HEALTH SYSTEMS CORPORATION.
Authorizes the Hawaii Health Systems Corporation (HHSC) to set rates for certain copies of records protected from the disclosure requirements of Chapter 92F, Hawaii Revised Statutes. Establishes minimum and maximum rates for copies of medical records subpoenaed from any of HHSC's regional system facilities, and requires the amount to be based on the actual cost of preparation. (HB1111 CD1)		 (S) 6/21/2016- Act 073, 06/20/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1174). BELATTI CPH/JDL, WAM
74 HB2009 HD1 SD1 CD1
Hoisting Machine Operators; Certification; Report
RELATING TO HOISTING MACHINE OPERATORS.
Authorizes the Hoisting Machine Operators Advisory Board (Board) to employ a 0.5 FTE office assistant instead of an executive director. Allows the Hoisting Machine Operators' Certification Revolving Fund to be used for consultants as necessary, instead of for the executive director. Repeals the position of the executive director for the Board. Requires the Board to submit a comprehensive report to the legislature no later than twenty days prior to the convening of the regular session of 2017. (HB2009 CD1)		 (S) 6/21/2016- Act 074, 06/20/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1175). NAKASHIMA JDL, WAM
75 HB1543 SD1 CD1
State Bonds ($)
RELATING TO STATE BONDS.
Authorizes issuance of general obligation bonds. Makes findings required by Article VII, section 13 of the State Constitution to declare that issuance of authorized bonds will not cause the debt limit to be exceeded. (HB1543 CD1)		 (S) 6/21/2016- Act 075, 06/20/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1176). LUKE WAM
76 SB2131 SD2 HD2 CD1
Naphtha Fuel; Fuel Tax; Power Generation; High Technology Special Fund
RELATING TO ENERGY.
Clarifies that naphtha fuel, used in a power-generating facility, is subject to the fuel tax at a rate of 2 cents per gallon retroactive to January 1, 2016. Clarifies the deposit requirements of the high technology special fund. (CD1)		 (S) 6/21/2016- Act 076, 06/20/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1177). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) EEP, FIN
77 SB2845 HD1 CD1
Appropriations; Kapalama Military Reservation; Hawaii Health Systems Corporation ($)
MAKING APPROPRIATIONS TO REPAY GENERAL FUND CASH ADVANCES.
Appropriates funds for fiscal year 20162017 to repay prior general fund cash advances for the purchase of lands of the former Kapalama Military Reservation and for the Hawaii Health Systems Corporation. (CD1)		 (S) 6/21/2016- Act 077, 06/20/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1178). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) FIN  HB2316
78 SB2910 SD2 HD1 CD1
Commissioners of Deeds; Rules; Fees; Administrative Fines; Office of the Lieutenant Governor; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO TIME SHARE COMMISSIONERS OF DEEDS.
Clarifies the Lieutenant Governor's rulemaking authority relating to commissioners of deeds. Authorizes the Lieutenant Governor to assess and collect fees and administrative fines. Clarifies that the Lieutenant Governor is responsible for the commissioners of deeds program. Increases the surety bond requirement for commissioners of deeds. Appropriates funds. (CD1)		 (S) 6/21/2016- Act 078, 06/20/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1179). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC, FIN  HB2381
79 HB2353 HD2 SD2 CD1
Position Exemptions; Civil Service
RELATING TO EXEMPTIONS FROM CIVIL SERVICE.
Makes various positions within the state energy office exempt from civil service. For a period of three years, authorizes the department of health to establish 18 exempt forensic psychologist positions and 2 exempt Hawaii state hospital primary care physician positions. For a period of three years, provides a civil service exemption for various positions in the department of human services, department of public safety, and the department of health. (HB2353 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 079, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1180). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) JDL, WAM  SB2882
80 HB2082 HD1 SD1
Adoption Records; Open Access
RELATING TO ADOPTION RECORDS.
Allows adopted individuals who have attained eighteen years of age, adoptive parents, and natural parents unfettered access to the adopted individual's sealed adoption records. (SD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 080, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1181). C. LEE, CULLEN, YAMANE JDL
81 HB27 SD1
Elections; Marked Ballot; Electronic or Digital Images
RELATING TO ELECTIONS.
Allows a voter to distribute or share an electronic or digital image of the voter's own marked ballot via social media or other means. Establishes that distributing or sharing the image of a voter's own marked ballot is not a defense for any election offenses or related offenses under the Penal Code. (SD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 081, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1182). RHOADS JDL
82 HB1705 SD1
Motor Vehicle Insurance; Motorcycle and Motor Scooter Insurance; Proof of Insurance; Electronic Motor Vehicle Insurance Card; Mobile Electronic Device
RELATING TO MOTOR VEHICLE INSURANCE.
Allows electronic insurance cards, in addition to paper insurance cards, to be used as proof of insurance for motor vehicles, motorcycles, and motor scooters. (SD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 082, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1183). MCKELVEY, ICHIYAMA CPH  SB2685
83 HB2281 SD1
Service of Process; Notice; Paternity Cases
RELATING TO SERVICE BY PUBLICATION IN PATERNITY CASES.
Amends the Uniform Parentage Act to allow for service by publication or by any other manner that is reasonably calculated to give the party actual notice of proceedings and an opportunity to be heard when respondents cannot be located or personally served. (SD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 083, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1184). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) JDL  SB2810
84 HB2295 HD1 SD1
Aerospace Advisory Committee; Membership; Chair
RELATING TO THE AEROSPACE ADVISORY COMMITTEE.
Reduces the number of members on the Aerospace Advisory Committee from 15 to 13. Allows the members of the Aerospace Advisory Committee to elect their own committee chair from members appointed by the Governor to this body. (SD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 084, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1185). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) PSM  SB2824
85 HB2311 SD1
Vital Records; Reporting Deaths to State Agencies
RELATING TO REPORTING DEATHS TO STATE AGENCIES.
Authorizes the Department of Health to continue to disclose, including through electronic means, lists of names of persons whose deaths have been recorded by DOH, to state agencies that maintain official lists of persons and are prohibited by federal law from sharing information for the lists. Requires lists of the deceased from DOH to also include the last four digits of the deceased's social security number, if known. (SD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 085, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1186). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) CPH, JDL  SB2840
86 HB2466 HD1 SD1
Cigarette Tax Revenues; Re-Distribution Study
RELATING TO NON-GENERAL FUNDS.
Requires DOH and BUF to conduct a study of the re-distribution of cigarette tax revenues. (SD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 086, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1187). LUKE WAM
87 HB2204 HD1 SD1
Department of Education; Teachers; Educational Officers; Salary Increases; Longevity Step Increases
RELATING TO EDUCATION.
Repeals annual increment and longevity step salary increases for department of education teachers and educational officers and clarifies that such increases shall be determined through collective bargaining. (SD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 087, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1188). TAKUMI, OHNO LAB, EDN, FIN  SB2622
88 HB2343 HD2 SD1 CD1
Child Care Providers; Background Checks; Health and Safety
RELATING TO REQUIREMENTS FOR CHILD CARE PROVIDERS.
Brings state law into alignment with changes to the federal Child Care and Development Block Grant, P.L. 113-186. Requires DHS to conduct background checks, including sex offender registry checks, on household members and all employees of child care facilities and providers that are licensed or registered by or receive subsidies from the State, to set health and safety standards, and enforce requirements. (HB2343 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 088, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1189). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) HMS/CPH, JDL  SB2872
89 HB1585 HD2 SD1 CD1
Uniform Probate Code; Guardianship; Adult; Rights; Communication
RELATING TO GUARDIANSHIP.
Prohibits guardians of an adult ward from restricting the ward's right to communicate, unless it is deemed by the guardian to pose a risk to the safety or well-being of the ward. (HB1585 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 089, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1190). MIZUNO JDL
90 SB2540 HD1 CD1
Department of Health Forensic Facility; Governor's Negotiation
RELATING TO THE DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH.
Authorizes the Governor, with the assistance of the Director of Health, to negotiate with any person for the development or expansion of a forensic facility of the Department of Health. Provides for an extended review and comment period for an environmental assessment or environmental impact statement. (CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 090, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1191). TOKUDA, BAKER, INOUYE, Dela Cruz, Gabbard HLT, FIN
91 SB2823 HD1
Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation; Executive Director Salary
RELATING TO THE HAWAII HOUSING FINANCE AND DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION.
Authorizes the Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation Board of Directors to set the salary of the Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation Executive Director in an amount not to exceed the salary of the Director of Business, Economic Development, and Tourism. (SB2823 HD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 091, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1192). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) HSG, FIN  HB2294
92 SB2861 SD2 HD1 CD1
Repeal; Joint Formulary Advisory Committee; Board of Nursing
RELATING TO THE JOINT FORMULARY ADVISORY COMMITTEE.
Repeals the Joint Formulary Advisory Committee established by the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs. Allows the Board of Nursing to be the sole authority that determines the exclusionary formulary for qualified advanced practice registered nurses with prescriptive authority. (CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 092, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1193). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) HLT, CPC, FIN  HB2332
93 HB2389 HD1 SD1 CD1
Federal Reimbursement Maximization Special Fund
RELATING TO THE FEDERAL REIMBURSEMENT MAXIMIZATION SPECIAL FUND.
Allows use of the Federal Reimbursement Maximization Special Fund to recruit and maintain corrections workforce. (HB2389 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 093, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1194). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) PSM, WAM  SB2918
94 HB900 HD2 SD1 CD1
Medical Assistance Fraud
RELATING TO MEDICAL ASSISTANCE FRAUD.
Provides that a person commits medical assistance fraud if the person knowingly makes or causes to be made a false statement or representation of a material fact relative to the medical assistance program. Clarifies that fraudulently applying for or renewing medical assistance benefits is a class C felony and specifies the restitution amount chargeable for a violation. Applies extended statute of limitations for felonies involving fraud or deception to medical assistance fraud. (HB900 CD1)		 (H) 6/22/2016- Act 094, on 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1195). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) HMS, JDL  SB1069
95 HB2093 HD1 SD2 CD1
Special Purpose Revenue Bonds; Goodwill Industries of Hawaii ($)
RELATING TO THE ISSUANCE OF SPECIAL PURPOSE REVENUE BONDS FOR GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF HAWAII, INC.
Authorizes the issuance of special purpose revenue bonds for projects of Goodwill Industries of Hawaii, Inc., and its affiliates. (HB2093 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 095, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1196). SAIKI, KEOHOKALOLE, LOPRESTI, SAN BUENAVENTURA EET, WAM
96 HB2340 HD1 SD2 CD1
Child Protective Act; Criminal History Record Checks
RELATING TO CRIMINAL HISTORY RECORDS CHECKS UNDER THE CHILD PROTECTIVE ACT.
Amends the Child Protective Act so that when an assessment is required, the Department of Human Services is authorized to conduct criminal history record checks, without consent, of an alleged perpetrator of harm or threat of harm and all adult household members living in the family home with the alleged victim child. (HB2340 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 096, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1197). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) HMS, JDL  SB2869
97 HB1756 HD3 SD2 CD1
Nursing; Licensure; Registration; Renewal; Criminal History Record Checks; Board of Nursing
RELATING TO NURSING.
Requires submittal of electronic fingerprints for criminal history record checks from nurse licensure applicants and nurse licensure renewal applicants. Authorizes the Board of Nursing to conduct investigations of applicants for licensure, renewal, and reactivation, request criminal history records of qualified applicants, and request criminal history records of persons issued licenses. (HB1756 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 097, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1198). BELATTI, MCKELVEY, MIZUNO HLT, CPC, JUD, FIN  SB2677
98 HB2231 HD1 SD1 CD1
Special Purpose Revenue Bonds; Energy Project; Project Party; Publicly Owned Energy Cooperative
RELATING TO ENERGY COOPERATIVES.
Clarifies that a publicly owned energy cooperative may be considered an energy project and project party for purposes of receiving financing through special purpose revenue bonds. (HB2231 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 098, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1199). LOWEN, BROWER, FUKUMOTO CHANG, ING, KOBAYASHI, C. LEE, LUKE, NISHIMOTO, OHNO, ONISHI, TAKAYAMA, THIELEN, TOKIOKA, WOODSON, YAMANE, YAMASHITA, McKelvey TRE/CPH, WAM
99 HB901 SD1 CD1
Law Enforcement Vehicles
RELATING TO LAW ENFORCEMENT VEHICLES.
Authorizes law enforcement vehicles of the harbors division of the department of transportation to be equipped with lights, sirens, and other equipment in a similar manner to other authorized emergency vehicles. (HB901 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 099, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1200). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) TRE, JDL  SB1070
100 HB1011 HD1 SD2 CD1
Traffic Code; Dangerous Wheels; Prohibited
RELATING TO THE TRAFFIC CODE.
Defines dangerous wheels and prohibits their use on vehicles, trailers, and semi-trailers. (HB1011 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 100, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1201). AQUINO, CULLEN, HAR, ICHIYAMA, JOHANSON, LOPRESTI, TAKAYAMA, TAKUMI, YAMANE, YAMASHITA, Nakashima, San Buenaventura TRE, JDL
101 HB2017 HD1 SD2 CD1
Workers' Compensation; Treatment Plans; Submission; Requirements
RELATING TO WORKERS' COMPENSATION TREATMENT PLANS.
Allows physicians to submit workers' compensation treatment plans to employers by mail or facsimile. Beginning January 1, 2021, requires employers to allow physicians to submit workers' compensation treatment plans to employers by mail, facsimile, or secure electronic means. Specifies requirements for receipt and acceptance of treatment plans. (HB2017 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 101, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1202). NAKASHIMA, KEOHOKALOLE CPH, JDL
102 HB2675 HD1 SD2 CD1
Rapid Ohia Death; Ceratocystis; Trees; Research; Study; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO RAPID OHIA DEATH.
Appropriates funds for research to combat rapid ohia death. (HB2675 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 102, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1203). ONISHI, AQUINO, BROWER, CACHOLA, CHOY, CREAGAN, CULLEN, DECOITE, EVANS, HAR, HASHEM, JOHANSON, KAWAKAMI, KEOHOKALOLE, KONG, LOWEN, LUKE, MATSUMOTO, MORIKAWA, OHNO, POUHA, RHOADS, SAIKI, SAN BUENAVENTURA, TAKAYAMA, TOKIOKA, TSUJI, WARD, WOODSON, YAMANE, Nakashima, Yamashita WLA, WAM
103 HB2657 HD2 SD2 CD1
Food Safety; Cost Reimbursement Program; Maui County Revolving Loan Program; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO AGRICULTURE.
Amends the Molokai diversified agricultural revolving loan program established by Act 387, SLH 1988, to provide cost reimbursements to Molokai farmers and ranchers to reimburse costs of mandated food safety compliance audits and other related costs. (HB2657 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 103, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1204). DECOITE, BROWER, ING, ITO, KEOHOKALOLE, ONISHI, POUHA, SOUKI, TSUJI, WOODSON, San Buenaventura WLA, WAM
104 HB2317 HD1 SD1 CD1
Appropriation; Emergency and Budget Reserve Fund ($)
MAKING AN APPROPRIATION TO THE EMERGENCY AND BUDGET RESERVE FUND.
Makes a general fund appropriation for fiscal year 20162017 to further capitalize the Emergency and Budget Reserve Fund. (HB2317 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 104, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1205). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) WAM  SB2846
105 HB2121 HD3 SD2 CD1
Appropriation; Judiciary; Legal Services ($)
RELATING TO LEGAL SERVICES.
Appropriates moneys from the general fund for civil legal services for low- and moderate-income persons to be purchased by the Judiciary. (HB2121 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 105, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1206). SOUKI, RHOADS JDL, WAM
106 HB2582 HD1 SD1 CD1
Agricultural Food Safety Certification Program; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO FOOD SAFETY.
Requires the Department of Agriculture to develop and implement an Agricultural Food Safety Certification Program that concurrently complies with the FDA Food Safety Modernization Act as it applies to farmers and ranchers. Authorizes and appropriates funds for program staff. (HB2582 CD1)		 (S) 6/22/2016- Act 106, 06/21/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1207). DECOITE, AQUINO, BROWER, CHOY, HAR, ITO, KEOHOKALOLE, LOWEN, MCKELVEY, NAKASHIMA, ONISHI, POUHA, SAY, TSUJI, WOODSON, YAMASHITA, Ing WLA/CPH, WAM
107 SB1311 SD2 HD1 CD1
Licensed Behavior Analyst; Practitioner; Direct Support Worker; Psychologist; Healthcare Professional
RELATING TO EDUCATION.
Exempts a licensed or credentialed practitioner practicing within the practitioner's own recognized scope of practice from licensure and certification requirements. Temporarily exempts from the licensing requirements for the practice of behavior analysis, direct support workers who provide autism treatment services pursuant to an Individualized Education Plan. Requires the Department of Education to report to the Legislature on implementing measures to comply with the licensure and certification requirements. (CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 107, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1208). TOKUDA, CHUN OAKLAND EDN, HLT/CPC, FIN  HB1440
108 SB2954 SD2 HD1
Criminal History Record Checks; Police; Permits to Acquire Firearms; Hawaii Criminal Justice Data Center; Database Management
RELATING TO FIREARMS.
Authorizes county police departments to enroll firearms applicants and individuals who are registering their firearms into a criminal record monitoring service used to alert police when an owner of a firearm is arrested for a criminal offense anywhere in the country. Authorizes the Hawaii Criminal Justice Data Center to access firearm registration data. (SB2954 HD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 108, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1209). ESPERO, Baker, Inouye, Nishihara JUD
109 HB625 HD1 SD1
Firearms Possession; Misdemeanor Stalking and Sexual Assault
RELATING TO FIREARMS.
Specifies that harassment by stalking and sexual assault are among the offenses that disqualify a person from owning, possessing, or controlling any firearm or ammunition. (SD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 109, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1210). C. LEE, Belatti, Evans, LoPresti PSM, JDL
110 HB2632 HD2 SD2 CD1
Firearms Disqualification; Mental Illness; Police
RELATING TO FIREARMS.
Requires firearms owners who have been disqualified from owning a firearm and ammunition due to a diagnosis of significant behavioral, emotional, or mental disorder, or due to emergency or involuntary hospitalization to a psychiatric facility, to immediately surrender their firearms and ammunition to the Chief of Police. Allows the Chief of Police to seize firearms and ammunition if a disqualified individual fails to surrender the firearms and ammunition after receiving written notice of the disqualification. (HB2632 CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 110, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1211). TAKAYAMA (Introduced by request of another party) PSM, JDL
111 HB799 HD2 SD1
Uniform Aeronautics Act; Wildlife Removal
RELATING TO THE UNIFORM AERONAUTICS ACT.
Clarifies the authority of the State, its employees and its independent contractors to kill or attempt to kill wildlife by aerial means in furtherance of official State duties. (SD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 111, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1212). ITO WLA, JDL
112 HB2267 HD1 SD1 CD1
Technology in Schools; Digital Format ($)
RELATING TO TECHNOLOGY IN SCHOOLS.
Defines curricular materials to include both printed and digital material. Clarifies that moneys for curricular materials can be used for digital formats. (HB2267 CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 112, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1213). OHNO, BROWER, EVANS, ING, ITO, JORDAN, KEOHOKALOLE, C. LEE, LUKE, MIZUNO, MORIKAWA, OSHIRO, TAKAYAMA, TAKUMI, TOKIOKA, YAMANE, Johanson, Kobayashi, San Buenaventura, Tsuji EDU, WAM  SB2606
113 HB2205 HD1 SD2 CD1
Charter Schools; Rules; Fees
RELATING TO CHARTER SCHOOLS.
Establishes requirements for public charter school board meetings. Clarifies that revocation and renewal proceedings shall not be subject to Chapter 91, Hawaii Revised Statutes. Authorizes charter schools to assess fees and charges for co-curricular activities. (HB2205 CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 113, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1214). TAKUMI, OHNO EDN, JUD, FIN  SB2780
114 HB2559 HD1 SD2 CD1
Mental Health; Assisted Community Treatment
RELATING TO MENTAL HEALTH.
Repeals the authority of a law enforcement officer to take into custody and transport a person subject to an assisted community treatment order to any designated mental health program. Requires a court order stating probable cause to believe a person is mentally ill or suffering from substance abuse, is imminently dangerous to self or others and in need of care or treatment, or both, to also direct the person to be taken into custody and, if subject to an assisted community treatment order, transported to any designated mental health program. Extends the maximum duration of assisted community treatment that may be ordered by the court to one year. Narrows the circumstances under which a subject of an assisted community treatment order may be physically forced to take medication to require the subject to be physically present in an emergency room or admitted to a hospital. Repeals the sunset date of the current statutory requirements for assisted community treatment. (HB2559 CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 114, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1215). RHOADS, BROWER, SAIKI HLT, JUD, FIN  SB2982
115 HB2233 HD2 SD1 CD1
Health Care Professionals; Email; Licensure; Infectious Diseases; Reporting
RELATING TO HEALTH CARE PROFESSIONALS.
Requires every physician, osteopathic physician, physician assistant, advanced practice registered nurse, and naturopathic physician to provide the applicable licensing authority with a current electronic mail address when applying for or renewing a license. Restricts the permissible disclosure and use of electronic mail addresses by licensing authorities. Requires that laboratory data reported to the Department of Health regarding an individual affected by or suspected of being affected by a communicable or dangerous disease or condition include the individual's complete demographic information. (HB2233 CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 115, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1216). LOWEN, BELATTI, CREAGAN, CULLEN, EVANS, FUKUMOTO CHANG, JORDAN, KEOHOKALOLE, KOBAYASHI, C. LEE, LUKE, MORIKAWA, NAKASHIMA, NISHIMOTO, ONISHI, OSHIRO, SAN BUENAVENTURA, SOUKI, TSUJI, YAMASHITA, Hashem HLT, CPC, FIN  SB3041
116 HB801 HD1 SD2 CD1
Special Purpose Revenue Bonds; SunStrong LLC; Renewable Energy Developer ($)
AUTHORIZING THE ISSUANCE OF SPECIAL PURPOSE REVENUE BONDS FOR SUNSTRONG LLC, A RENEWABLE ENERGY DEVELOPER SERVING THE GENERAL PUBLIC IN PROVIDING ELECTRIC ENERGY.
Authorizes the issuance of special purpose revenue bonds to SunStrong LLC, a renewable energy developer, for the provision of electric energy and installation of renewable energy projects in Hawaii. (HB801 CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 116, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1217). ITO TRE, WAM  SB1042
117 HB2593 HD1 SD1
Special Purpose Revenue Bonds; Food Crop Production; Animal Feed Production; Renewable Energy; Hawaii Renewable Resources, LLC; O‘ahu ($)
AUTHORIZING THE ISSUANCE OF SPECIAL PURPOSE REVENUE BONDS FOR HAWAII RENEWABLE RESOURCES, LLC.
Authorizes the issuance of special purpose revenue bonds in the amount of $30,000,000 to assist Hawaii Renewable Resources, LLC, with the development of facilities for food crop, animal feed, and renewable non-fossil fuel production on O‘ahu. (SD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 117, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1218). SOUKI TRE/WLA, WAM
118 HB2669 HD1 SD1
Special Purpose Revenue Bonds; TruTag Technologies, Inc. ($)
RELATING TO THE ISSUANCE OF SPECIAL PURPOSE REVENUE BONDS TO ASSIST TRUTAG TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
Authorizes the issuance of special purpose revenue bonds for TruTag Technologies, Inc. (SD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 118, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1219). KAWAKAMI, AQUINO, CULLEN, MORIKAWA, TOKIOKA EET, WAM  SB2506
119 HB545 HD1 SD2
Non-General Funds; Transfer; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO FUNDS.
Transfers excess revenues from the bureau of conveyances special fund to the Hawaii historic preservation special fund. Makes an appropriation for an integrated information management system and document digitization for the State's historic preservation division. Effective 6/29/2016. (SD2)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 119, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1220). CHOY, ICHIYAMA, KONG, LOPRESTI, SAY HED, WAL, FIN
120 HB2403 HD1 SD1
Medical Advisory Board; Composition
RELATING TO THE MEDICAL ADVISORY BOARD.
Amends the composition of the medical advisory board. (SD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 120, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1221). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) CPH/TRE  SB2932
121 HB1684 HD2 SD1 CD1
Natural Energy Laboratory of Hawaii Authority
RELATING TO THE NATURAL ENERGY LABORATORY OF HAWAII AUTHORITY.
Exempts the operations of the Natural Energy Laboratory of Hawaii Authority from bidding requirements for concessions or space on public property. (HB1684 CD1)		 (H) 6/23/2016- Act 121, on 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1222). C. LEE EDB, FIN
122 SB2850 SD2 HD1 CD1
Division of Financial Institutions; Mortgage Loan Originators; Mortgage Servicers; Secure and Fair Enforcement for Mortgage Licensing Act
RELATING TO MORTGAGE INDUSTRY REGULATION.
Amends laws pertaining to mortgage loan originators and mortgage loan servicers by clarifying the applicable scope of each chapter, making terminology more consistent across both chapters, and updating references to and provisions implementing controlling federal law. (CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 122, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1223). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC, FIN  HB2321
123 SB2670 HD1 CD1
Licensing; Behavior Analysis; Behavior Analysts; Surcharge; License Fee; Renewal Fee; Direct Support Workers
RELATING TO BEHAVIOR ANALYSTS.
Reduces the surcharge on the license fee and renewal fee for behavior analysts from $100 to $50. Temporarily exempts from the licensing requirements for the practice of behavior analysis direct support workers who provide Medicaid home and community-based services pursuant to the Social Security Act through January 1, 2019. (CD1)		 (S) 6/23/2016- Act 123, 06/22/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1224). BAKER, KIDANI, Riviere HLT/CPC, FIN  HB1856
124 HB1700 HD1 SD1 CD1
State budget. ($)
RELATING TO THE STATE BUDGET.
To adjust and request appropriations for Fiscal Biennium 201517 funding requirements for operations and capital improvement projects of Executive Branch agencies and programs.		 (H) 6/24/2016- Act 124, on 06/23/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1225). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) WAM  SB2136
125 SB2647 SD1 HD2
Wildlife; Species; Trafficking; Fines
RELATING TO NATURAL RESOURCES.
Prohibits the sale, offer to sell, purchase, trade, possession with intent to sell, or barter of any part or product from various animal and marine species. Provides exceptions to this prohibition, including for traditional cultural practices protected under the State Constitution. Imposes penalties for violations of the prohibition on trafficking animal parts and products. (SB2647 HD2)		 (H) 6/24/2016- Act 125, on 06/23/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1226). GABBARD, Kim WAL, JUD
126 HB2501 HD2 SD2 CD1
Water Rights; Holdover
RELATING TO WATER RIGHTS.
Requires that where an application has been made for a lease to continue a previously authorized disposition of water rights, a holdover may be authorized annually until the pending application for the disposition of water rights is finally resolved or for a total of three consecutive one-year holdovers, whichever occurs sooner. (HB2501 CD1)		 (S) 6/28/2016- Act 126, 06/27/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1228). YAMANE, SOUKI, TSUJI, WOODSON, YAMASHITA WAL, JUD, FIN  SB3001
127 SB2561 SD2 HD1 CD1
Affordable Rental Housing Goal; Special Action Team on Rental Housing; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO RENTAL HOUSING.
Establishes a goal of developing or vesting the development of at least 22,500 affordable rental housing units ready for occupancy between 01/01/17 and 12/31/26. Establishes a temporary special action team on rental housing to make recommendations to the Governor, Legislature, and other parties to achieve the goal. Makes an appropriation. (CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 127, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1229). TOKUDA, BAKER, CHUN OAKLAND, DELA CRUZ, ENGLISH, GALUTERIA, INOUYE, KIDANI, KOUCHI, NISHIHARA HSG, FIN
128 SB2566 SD1 HD1 CD1
Rental Housing Revolving Fund; Rental Assistance Revolving Fund; Transfer
RELATING TO HOUSING.
Transfers excess moneys from the rental assistance revolving fund to the rental housing revolving fund. (CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 128, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1230). TOKUDA, DELA CRUZ, ENGLISH, GALUTERIA, INOUYE, KOUCHI, NISHIHARA, Baker, Kidani HSG, FIN
129 SB2833 SD2 HD2 CD1
Low-income Housing Tax Credit
RELATING TO THE LOW-INCOME HOUSING TAX CREDIT.
Increases funding for affordable rental housing development by making the State Low-Income Housing Tax Credit more valuable. Reduces State Tax Credit period from ten to five years. Repeals 12/31/2021. (CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 129, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1231). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) HSG, FIN  HB2304
130 SB3077 SD1 HD1 CD1
Hawaii Interagency Council for Transit-oriented Development; Established; Coordinated Statewide Planning; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO STATEWIDE COMMUNITY PLANNING.
Designates the Office of Planning as the lead state agency for transit-oriented development coordination. Establishes the Hawaii Interagency Council for Transit-oriented Development within DBEDT to coordinate effective and efficient transit-oriented development planning on a statewide level. Appropriates moneys. Allows the Department of Education to use school impact fees from projects within a county-designated transit oriented development zone for various purposes, including construction of new school facilities in new or existing sites statewide. (CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 130, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1232). HARIMOTO, CHUN OAKLAND, ESPERO, GALUTERIA, GREEN, INOUYE, NISHIHARA, Ihara, Keith-Agaran, Kouchi, Shimabukuro, Taniguchi, L. Thielen, Wakai TRN/WAL, FIN
131 HB2293 HD1 SD1
Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation; Mixed-use developments
RELATING TO THE HAWAII HOUSING FINANCE AND DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION.
Enables the Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation to develop mixed-use developments in partnership with state and county departments and agencies. Eff. 7/1/2016. (SD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 131, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1233). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) HOU/PSM, WAM  SB2822
132 HB2305 HD1 SD1 CD1
Dwelling Unit Revolving Fund; Regional State Infrastructure Improvement Subaccounts
RELATING TO THE DWELLING UNIT REVOLVING FUND.
Authorizes the creation of Regional State Infrastructure Improvement Subaccounts within the Dwelling Unit Revolving Fund and the use of the Dwelling Unit Revolving Fund to provide loans and grants to finance regional state infrastructure improvements in areas of planned growth. (HB2305 CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 132, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1234). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) HOU, WAM  SB2834
133 HB2350 HD2 SD1 CD1
Foster Children
RELATING TO FOSTER CHILDREN.
Updates the wording from "foster boarding home" to "resource family home" and "foster parent" to "resource caregiver"; establishes qualified immunity for resource caregivers and child caring institutions adhering to the reasonable and prudent parent standard; adds the requirement that resource caregivers and child care institutions use the reasonable and prudent parent standard when authorizing children in foster care to participate in activities; and changes the age from sixteen to fourteen years of age for children in foster care to be informed of services to transition from foster care to independent living. (HB2350 CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 133, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1235). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) HMS, JDL  SB2879
134 SB2878 SD1 HD1
Youth Transitioning From Foster Care; Deadline Extension; Higher Education Financial Assistance
RELATING TO YOUTH TRANSITIONING FROM FOSTER CARE.
Extends the application deadline for financial assistance for higher education available to foster or former foster youth, clarifies that financial assistance is for related higher education costs, and clarifies eligibility and program requirements of the Young Adult Voluntary Foster Care Program. (SB2878 HD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 134, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1236). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) HUS, FIN  HB2349
135 HB2084 HD2 SD1
Health Insurance; Insurers; Participation and Coverage; Non-discrimination; Gender Identity
RELATING TO INSURANCE.
Prohibits all insurers in the State, including health insurers, mutual benefit societies, health maintenance organizations, and health benefits plans under chapter 87A, Hawaii Revised Statutes, from discriminating with respect to participation and coverage under a policy, contract, plan, or agreement against any person on the basis of a person's actual gender identity or perceived gender identity. (SD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 135, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1237). C. LEE, BELATTI, CULLEN, ING, LOWEN, YAMANE CPH, JDL  SB2666
136 HB1943 HD1 SD1 CD1
Long-term Care Facilities; Cost Increase; Inflationary Adjustment; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO LONG-TERM CARE FACILITIES.
Provides an inflationary adjustment to the methodology used to reimburse facilities for the long-term care of medicaid recipients for fiscal year 2016-2017. Makes an appropriation. (HB1943 CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 136, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1238). BELATTI, CREAGAN, KOBAYASHI, MIZUNO, MORIKAWA HMS/CPH, WAM  SB2396
137 SB2076 SD2 HD1 CD1
Durable Medical Equipment; Licensure; Office of Health Care Assurance Special Fund
RELATING TO HEALTH CARE.
Establishes a durable medical equipment supplier license program for suppliers of durable medical equipment, prosthetics, orthotics, and related supplies through the office of health care assurance. Allows license fees to be deposited into the office of health care assurance special fund. Amends the cap on amounts of the office of health care assurance special fund that may be used per fiscal year. Amends the balance of the office of health care assurance special fund above which moneys are deposited into the general fund. Takes effect on 1/1/2017. (CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 137, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1239). CHUN OAKLAND, IHARA, Galuteria, Nishihara, L. Thielen HLT, CPC, FIN  HB1673
138 HB1878 HD1 SD2 CD1
Aging and Disability Resource Centers; Fall Prevention; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO AGING.
Appropriates funds for Aging and Disability Resource Centers (ADRCs) and for fall prevention and early detection services for the elderly. Requires the Executive Office on Aging to submit a report to the Legislature no later than 60 days prior to the convening of the Regular Sessions of 2017 and 2018 on its progress to develop a system of evaluation to determine the effectiveness of the ADRCs in each county and to implement the federal No Wrong Door/ADRC network implementation grant. (HB1878 CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 138, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1240). TAKAYAMA, CACHOLA, CHOY, CREAGAN, DECOITE, EVANS, HAR, ICHIYAMA, JORDAN, KAWAKAMI, KEOHOKALOLE, LOPRESTI, LOWEN, MIZUNO, MORIKAWA, NISHIMOTO, OHNO, OSHIRO, SAY, THIELEN, TOKIOKA, WOODSON, Yamashita HMS/CPH, WAM  SB2085
139 HB2482 HD2 SD1 CD1
State Health Planning and Development Agency; Health and Health Care Information and Data
RELATING TO INSURANCE.
Broadens the scope of health and health care data and other information submitted to the state health planning and development agency. (CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 139, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1241). BELATTI, JOHANSON, LUKE, NISHIMOTO, Rhoads HLT, CPC, FIN
140 SB2853 SD2 HD1 CD1
Insurance; Risk Management and Own Risk and Solvency Assessment Act; Captive Insurance Companies; Model Risk Retention Act
RELATING TO INSURANCE.
Adopts the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) Risk Management and Own Risk and Solvency Assessment Model Act. Applies certain requirements for risk-based capital provisions, severability, and notice provisions to risk retention captive insurance companies. Incorporates revisions to the NAIC Model Risk Retention Act. (CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 140, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1242). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC, FIN  HB2324
141 SB2854 SD2 HD1 CD1
Property Insurance; Premium Tax; Electronic Filing; Electronic Payments; Short-term Health Policies; Child Health Supervision Services; Claim Filer; Risk Retention; Captive Insurance; Mental Illness Benefits; Duty to Respond; Visual or Optometric Services Coverage; Surgical or Emergency Services Coverage; Contraceptive Services
RELATING TO INSURANCE.
Makes various amendments in the Insurance Code relating to property insurance, market conduct, insurance premium taxes, insurance contracts, accident and health or sickness insurance, claim filing, captive insurance companies, duty to respond, mental health and alcohol and drug abuse treatment insurance benefits, Hawaii hurricane relief fund, covered services, mutual benefit societies, and health maintenance organizations. (CD1)		 (H) 6/30/2016- Act 141, on 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1243). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC, FIN  HB2325
142 HB2326 HD1 SD1
Mortgage Rescue Fraud Prevention Act; Mortgage Rescue Fraud; Mortgage Assistance Relief Services; Distressed Property Owners
RELATING TO MORTGAGE RESCUE FRAUD.
Makes numerous amendments to Hawaii's mortgage rescue fraud prevention act, chapter 480E, Hawaii Revised Statutes, for consistency with the federal Mortgage Assistance Relief Services Rule. (SD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 142, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1244). SOUKI (Introduced by request of another party) CPH, JDL  SB2855
143 HB1894 HD1 SD1
Post-Secondary Education; Authorization; Closed Institution Records
RELATING TO POST-SECONDARY EDUCATION.
Clarifies that under the Hawaii Post-Secondary Education Authorization Program, when an institution ceases to operate, the student transcripts shall be kept permanently in a form prescribed by the director and other records requested and obtained by DCCA may be disposed of at the director's discretion. (SD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 143, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1245). ICHIYAMA HEA, CPH
144 HB1096 HD2 SD1
Residential Mortgage Loans; Mortgage Loan Originator Companies; Posted Business Hours; Regular Business Hours
RELATING TO THE SECURE AND FAIR ENFORCEMENT FOR MORTGAGE LICENSING ACT.
Specifies that mortgage loan originator companies shall be open for business to the public during posted business hours, at least some of which shall be during regular business hours. (SD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 144, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1246). MCKELVEY (Introduced by request of another party) CPH
145 HB1669 HD2 SD1
Public Accountancy; Peer Review; Peer Review Compliance Reporting Forms; Peer Review Oversight Committee
RELATING TO PUBLIC ACCOUNTANCY.
Redefines "peer review" to include reviews done by out-of-state public accountancy licensees. Increases deadlines for filing of peer review compliance reporting forms and appealing certain ratings from 10 days to 30 calendar days. Allows members of the public accountancy peer review oversight committee to be reimbursed for expenses and protects the members from civil liability for actions done in furtherance of the purposes of the committee. Defines the term "Hawaii attest work". (SD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 145, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1247). CHOY CPH, WAM  SB2407
146 SB2863 SD2 HD1 CD1
Massage Therapy; Massage Therapists; Massage Therapist Apprentices; Licensees; Violations; Citation; Fines
RELATING TO CITATIONS FOR MASSAGE THERAPY VIOLATIONS.
Authorizes the issuance of a citation for specific violations of massage therapy laws or rules by licensees. Establishes a process for licensees to contest the citation. Provides for the assessment of fines for each violation. (CD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 146, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1248). KOUCHI (Introduced by request of another party) CPC, JUD, FIN  HB2334
147 SB2671 SD1 HD2 CD1
Veterinary Technicians; Veterinary Technology Practice; Registration; Titles; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO VETERINARY TECHNICIANS.
Beginning 7/1/2018, establishes requirements for the regulation of veterinary technicians and the practice of veterinary technology. Establishes registration requirements and limitations on use of titles for veterinary technicians. Appropriates funds to implement the registration of veterinary technicians. (CD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 147, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1249). BAKER, KIDANI, Gabbard, Taniguchi CPC, FIN  HB1955
148 SB2673 SD2 HD2 CD1
Travel Agency; Activity Desk; Activities; Registration
RELATING TO TRAVEL AGENCIES.
Requires a travel agency that sells activities individually or as part of a tour package to register as an activity desk. (CD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 148, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1250). BAKER, KIDANI, Wakai TOU, CPC
149 SB2680 SD1 HD1 CD1
Division of Financial Institutions; Financial Institutions; Fee Schedule
RELATING TO FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS.
Clarifies and addresses inconsistencies within the fee schedule for financial institutions in chapter 412, Hawaii Revised Statutes. Repeals the credit union advisory board. (CD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 149, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1251). BAKER, KIDANI, Espero, Riviere, Shimabukuro, Tokuda CPC, FIN
150 HB1608 HD1 SD1 CD1
UH; Graduation Pathway System; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO THE UNIVERSITY OF HAWAII.
Appropriates $750,000 for the UH Graduation Pathway System. (HB1608 CD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 150, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1252). CHOY HEA, WAM
151 HB1814 HD1 SD1 CD1
Department of Education; Pupil Punishment; Physical Restraints; Appropriation ($)
RELATING TO EDUCATION.
Appropriates funds for a full time position to provide training and track data on public school pupil punishment and use of restraints restrictions established by Act 206, Session Laws of Hawaii 2014. Requires report. Appropriates funds. (HB1814 CD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 151, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1253). TAKUMI, OHNO EDU, WAM  SB2611
152 SB2981 SD1 HD1 CD1
American Sign Language; Official World Language; Public Schools; World Language Requirement
RELATING TO EDUCATION.
Recognizes American Sign Language as a world language, and allows public school students who pass courses in American Sign Language to receive credit toward world language graduation requirements. (CD1)		 (S) 6/30/2016- Act 152, 06/29/2016 (Gov. Msg. No. 1254). BAKER, KIDANI EDN, FIN
153 HB1044 HD1 SD1
Sexual Assault in the Second Degree; Sexual Assault in the Fourth Degree; Sexual Penetration; Sexual Contact; Minor
RELATING TO SEXUAL ASSAULT.
Amends the offense of sexual assault in the second degree to include a person who knowingly subjects to sexual penetration with a minor at least sixteen years of age and the person is contemporaneously acting in a professional capacity to instruct, advise, or supervise the minor, with certain exceptions.
